January.description: Winter is in full swing, with heavy snowfalls and cold temperatures. The trail is difficult, and travel is especially challenging in the mountains.

February.description: The winter chill persists, and snow is common. Snowstorms can disrupt travel, and the cold can be biting.

March.description: Spring begins to stir, but rain and occasional snowstorms still slow progress. The weather is unpredictable, with chilly mornings and milder afternoons.

April.description: April showers bring plenty of rain, and thunderstorms become more common. Temperatures are rising, and the trail becomes muddier.

May.description: May brings more stable weather, though rain still comes in bursts. Winds are common, and it’s a good time to move, though the days are warm and sunny.

June.description: Early summer sees clear skies and rising temperatures. Some breezy days provide relief, but it’s a hot stretch of the journey.

July.description: The peak of summer arrives with sweltering heat. The sun beats down relentlessly, and while the winds help, it’s a grueling time to travel.

August.description: Hot summer days persist. The heat continues, but the nights provide some relief as the temperature drops slightly.

September.description: The warmth of summer fades into early autumn. Days are still mild, but rain becomes more frequent as the trail progresses.

October.description: Autumn sets in with cooler temperatures and increasing rainfall. Snow may begin to appear in higher elevations.

November.description: Snow becomes more common, especially at higher elevations. The cold and storms make travel more difficult.

December.description: Winter sets in with heavy snowfalls and cold temperatures. Travel becomes especially challenging in the mountains.
